topic_text: Resolved: If all mammals are vertebrates and all whales are mammals, then all whales are vertebrates.
rationale: Valid Barbara syllogism: All M are P; All S are M; therefore All S are P. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If some birds can fly and penguins are birds, then penguins can fly.
rationale: Existential fallacy: 'some birds fly' does not entail all birds fly. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If no reptiles are warm-blooded and all snakes are reptiles, then no snakes are warm-blooded.
rationale: Valid Celarent syllogism. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If all philosophers are thinkers and some thinkers are mathematicians, then some philosophers are mathematicians.
rationale: Undistributed middle: the mathematicians could be non-philosopher thinkers. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If it is raining then the ground is wet; the ground is wet, therefore it is raining.
rationale: Affirming the consequent fallacy. Many things besides rain make ground wet. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If all prime numbers greater than 2 are odd and 17 is a prime number greater than 2, then 17 is odd.
rationale: Valid Barbara syllogism with true premises. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If P implies Q and Q is false, then P is false.
rationale: Valid modus tollens. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If every student passed the exam and John is not a student, then John did not pass the exam.
rationale: Denying the antecedent: John's non-student status says nothing about whether he passed. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If all squares are rectangles and all rectangles are quadrilaterals, then all squares are quadrilaterals.
rationale: Valid Barbara syllogism with true premises. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: If most humans have two legs and most creatures with two legs are birds, then most humans are birds.
rationale: Majoritarian fallacy: 'most' does not combine transitively. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The concept of an omnipotent being who can create a stone so heavy that it cannot lift leads to a logical contradiction.
rationale: Classical omnipotence paradox: claim is self-contradictory. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The statement 'this sentence is false' has a consistent truth value.
rationale: Liar paradox: no consistent truth assignment exists. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A set that contains all sets which do not contain themselves leads to a logical contradiction.
rationale: Russell's paradox: demonstrates naive set theory is inconsistent. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A barber who shaves exactly those men who do not shave themselves can consistently exist.
rationale: Barber paradox: premise is self-contradictory. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Under single-timeline models of time travel, preventing one's own birth creates a logical contradiction.
rationale: Grandfather paradox correctly identifies contradiction in single-timeline models. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The statement 'nothing is absolutely true' is itself absolutely true.
rationale: Self-refuting statement: if true, it is false. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Given that removing one grain from a heap leaves a heap, removing all grains one-by-one still leaves a heap.
rationale: Sorites paradox: vague predicates resist unlimited induction; zero grains is not a heap. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Zeno's dichotomy paradox proves that all motion is impossible.
rationale: Resolved by convergent infinite series: motion is possible. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A conscious observer can simultaneously know the exact position and exact momentum of a quantum particle.
rationale: Heisenberg uncertainty: precision in position and momentum are fundamentally bounded. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The notion of 'the set of all sets' leads to contradictions in modern set theory.
rationale: Cantor's paradox: universal set produces size contradictions. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The claim 'vaccines cause autism because one celebrity's child showed symptoms after vaccination' is a sound argument.
rationale: Anecdotal + post hoc fallacies. Large studies find no causal link. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The argument 'climate science is wrong because some 1970s scientists predicted cooling' is logically valid.
rationale: Misrepresents 1970s science and cherry-picks. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A claim is false if its proponent is unqualified to defend it.
rationale: Ad hominem fallacy: proponent's qualifications are irrelevant to truth. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A valid logical argument should reliably produce a true conclusion when its premises are true.
rationale: Definition of logical validity. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: 'Smoking is harmless because one person smoked for 70 years and lived to 95' is a well-founded conclusion.
rationale: Anecdotal evidence cannot refute statistical evidence. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Evaluating a treatment's causal effect requires a control group or counterfactual comparison.
rationale: Core principle of causal inference. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: 'Either support this policy completely or you are an enemy of the state' is a legitimate rhetorical framing.
rationale: False dilemma: ignores intermediate positions. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: A scientific theory should be evaluated on its evidence rather than the motives of its proponents.
rationale: Basic epistemic principle: genetic fallacy avoidance. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: 'If we ban plastic straws, we must ban all plastics, causing economic collapse' follows valid reasoning.
rationale: Slippery slope fallacy: no causal chain established. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The number of people who hold a belief is sufficient to determine whether that belief is true.
rationale: Ad populum fallacy: popularity does not establish truth. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: There are infinitely many prime numbers.
rationale: Proven by Euclid c. 300 BCE. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Zero is an even number.
rationale: 0 = 2 * 0, satisfying definition of even. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The square root of 2 is a rational number.
rationale: Classical proof by contradiction establishes irrationality. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The decimal 0.999... (repeating) equals exactly 1.
rationale: Limit of convergent geometric series equals 1. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Every positive integer greater than 1 has a unique prime factorization up to ordering.
rationale: Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: There exists a largest prime number.
rationale: Euclid's proof of infinitude of primes refutes this. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The sum of interior angles of any triangle in Euclidean geometry is exactly 180 degrees.
rationale: Classical theorem for flat space. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Division by zero is well-defined in standard real-number arithmetic.
rationale: Division by zero is undefined. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Every continuous function on a closed interval attains its maximum and minimum values.
rationale: Extreme Value Theorem (Weierstrass). Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The cardinality of the real numbers equals the cardinality of the natural numbers.
rationale: Cantor's diagonal argument shows reals are strictly larger (uncountable). Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The Earth orbits the Sun rather than the reverse.
rationale: Established by centuries of observation and Newtonian gravity. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Humans only use 10 percent of their brains.
rationale: Neuroimaging shows activity throughout the brain. Common myth. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm).
rationale: Celsius scale defined such that water boils at 100C at 1 atm. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
rationale: Not visible from Moon and barely from low Earth orbit. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Antibiotics are effective against viral infections such as the common cold.
rationale: Antibiotics target bacteria, not viruses. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Light travels faster than sound under standard terrestrial conditions.
rationale: Light ~3e8 m/s vs sound ~343 m/s in air. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: All known living organisms use DNA as their primary genetic material.
rationale: RNA viruses (flu, coronaviruses, HIV) use RNA. 'All' is too strong. Con is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The speed of light in vacuum is the same in all inertial reference frames.
rationale: Second postulate of special relativity, experimentally confirmed. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: Evolution by natural selection is the primary mechanism driving biological diversity.
rationale: Supported by fossil record, genetics, observed speciation. Pro is correct.

topic_text: Resolved: The universe is approximately 13.8 billion years old.
rationale: From CMB measurements and Hubble constant. Pro is correct.
